name: avahi-utils
title: avahi-utils
summary: Avahi browsing, publishing and discovery utilities
description:
Avahi browsing, publishing and discovery utilities
Avahi is a fully LGPL framework for Multicast DNS Service Discovery.
It allows programs to publish and discover services and hosts
running on a local network with no specific configuration.  For
example you can plug into a network and instantly find printers to
print to, files to look at and people to talk to.
.
This package contains several utilities that allow you to interact with
the Avahi daemon, including publish, browsing and discovering services.

commands.name: avahi-browse
commands.description: Browse for mDNS/DNS-SD services using the Avahi daemon

commands.name: avahi-resolve
commands.description: Resolve one or more mDNS/DNS host name(s) to IP address(es) (and vice versa) using the Avahi daemon

commands.description: Browse for mDNS/DNS-SD services using the Avahi daemon

commands.name: avahi-resolve-address

commands.description: Resolve one or more mDNS/DNS host name(s) to IP address(es) (and vice versa) using the Avahi daemon

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use avahi-browse because Avahi browsing, publishing and discovery utilities.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use avahi-browse as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `avahi-browse [options] service-type` as documented upstream for avahi-browse.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `avahi-browse [options] --all` as documented upstream for avahi-browse.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `avahi-browse [options] --browse-domains` as documented upstream for avahi-browse.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `avahi-browse [options] --dump-db` as documented upstream for avahi-browse.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair avahi-browse with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `avahi-browse --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
